settings.designer_1_description: Justine Nettleton creates enamel jewellery as 830 Degrees from her Nottingham studio. With a background in Fine Art, her small-batch pieces celebrate material transformation and expressive surface design. Using kiln-fired enamel fused onto copper, she builds colour and texture, as each firing produces subtle variations in tone and surface, making every piece unique.
settings.designer_2_description: Gwawr Jones founded Deryn Jewellery in 2018, creating colourful, handcrafted pieces from her studio in North Wales. Inspired by eclectic styling, collected materials, and natural colour combinations, each piece is made individually in small batches, celebrating playful design.
settings.designer_3_description: Jenni Douglas is an Edinburgh-based artist and designer working across jewellery, prints, and homeware. Inspired by mid-century design, her colourful work combines graphic pattern with thoughtful small-scale production.
settings.designer_4_description: Will Sharp is a contemporary jeweller and Glasgow School of Art graduate. He creates geometric jewellery in recycled silver and plant-based bio-plastics. His process merges digital innovation with traditional bench skills to produce sculptural, wearable forms. Designed using CAD software and finished by hand, the result is a contemporary range of jewellery that feels modern and invigorating.
settings.designer_5_description:
Mia Houghton, founder of Mijoux Creations, handcrafts jewellery in silver using traditional bench techniques alongside enamel. Inspired by nature, her Salisbury-based studio practice focuses on small-batch making. 
Handcrafted, her jewellery reflects Mia’s interest in colour, movement, and geometry. Traditional bench techniques and careful finishing give her pieces a delicate and confident presence.
settings.designer_6_description: Leah Lewis founded the Natural Paper Company from a lifelong love of paper and stationery. Traditional craft processes and sustainable materials sit at the heart of the work, using natural, textured paper chosen for its tactile quality and durability. Thoughtfully produced using sustainable paper sources, their products invite slow creativity and everyday use.
settings.designer_7_description: Claire founded Copper Bee in 2019, handcrafting jewellery from her Wiltshire studio. Working primarily with copper and mixed metals, she uses traditional forming and texturing techniques to create warm, tactile surfaces. Her small-batch pieces celebrate colour, individuality, and the character of hand-worked materials.
settings.designer_8_description: Kirsty Thomas founded Tom Pigeon, a Scottish design studio creating jewellery in small batches. Inspired by modern art and architectural colour blocking, the designs balance bold colour with simple shapes, and each one functions as a small piece of wearable sculpture. Kirsty pays very careful attention to colour and form in her pieces.
